name: Help for function with arguments block
description: Tests that the skill correctly reads an arguments block to determine required, optional, and name-value inputs, and produces properly structured syntax paragraphs
prompt:
Write help text for the function in evals/files/interpolateData.m

The function interpolates yData at queryPoints using the specified method. The method argument accepts exactly these values: "linear", "nearest", "next", "previous", "spline", "pchip". The Tolerance name-value is a numeric scalar (not an enumeration). The ExtrapolationMethod name-value accepts a string but the valid values are not known from the code — do NOT invent a list.

assertions.description: H1 line preserves camelCase and uses space-dash-space separator
assertions.expected: %interpolateData - — name is not uppercased because it contains uppercase letters, and uses ' - ' (space-dash-space) after the name. FAILURE looks like: %INTERPOLATEDATA, or %interpolateData  (two spaces), or %interpolateData: (colon)

assertions.description: First syntax paragraph shows only required args
assertions.expected: First syntax shows 'RESULT = interpolateData(xData, yData, queryPoints)' or 'RESULT = interpolateData(xData,yData,queryPoints)' with only the three required positional args. FAILURE looks like: including METHOD or Tolerance in the first syntax

assertions.description: Optional arg METHOD gets its own syntax paragraph
assertions.expected: METHOD appears in a separate syntax paragraph from the required args (e.g., 'interpolateData(...,METHOD)' or 'interpolateData(xData,yData,queryPoints,METHOD)'). FAILURE looks like: METHOD combined in the first syntax with required args, or METHOD not mentioned in any syntax

assertions.description: Name-value args shown with Name=VALUE syntax preserving original casing
assertions.expected: Name-value pair uses 'Tolerance=' with original PascalCase preserved. FAILURE looks like: TOLERANCE= (uppercased), or tolerance= (lowercased)

assertions.description: No comment line exceeds 75 characters measured from the %
assertions.expected: Every line from % to end-of-line is at most 75 characters. FAILURE looks like: a description line that extends beyond 75 chars from the %, even if it fits in 80 columns total

assertions.description: Help indentation matches body code indentation
assertions.expected: Body code is indented 4 spaces, so help comments must also be indented 4 spaces (i.e., '    %interpolateData'). FAILURE looks like: '%interpolateData' at column 1 while body code starts at column 5

assertions.description: METHOD flag values listed with dash-alignment, default first with (default) at start
assertions.expected: METHOD has enumerated values listed as double-quoted strings with ' - ' separator, dashes aligned, default value listed FIRST with (default) at the START of its description. FAILURE looks like: (default) at end of line, or default not listed first, or values not aligned

assertions.description: No block comments used

name: Help for a classdef file
description: Tests that the skill generates class-level help with construction syntax, method listings, and property listings
prompt:
Write help text for the class in evals/files/SignalBuffer.m

The class stores audio signal data with metadata and provides trimming and duration queries.

assertions.description: H1 line uses the class name as-is with space-dash-space separator
assertions.expected: %SignalBuffer - — name preserves MixedCase and uses ' - ' (space-dash-space). FAILURE looks like: %SIGNALBUFFER, or %SignalBuffer  (two spaces), or missing separator

assertions.description: Construction syntax uses correct casing for class name and variables
assertions.expected: Construction syntax shows 'OBJ = SignalBuffer(DATA)' — obj is all-lowercase so it becomes OBJ, SignalBuffer preserves MixedCase, data is all-lowercase so it becomes DATA. FAILURE looks like: obj = SignalBuffer(data) (not uppercased), or OBJ = SIGNALBUFFER(DATA) (class name uppercased)

assertions.description: Method listing uses 'functions:' not 'methods:'
assertions.expected: SignalBuffer functions:

assertions.description: Private method validateIndex must NOT appear in listings
assertions.expected: The method listing (under 'SignalBuffer functions:') does NOT include validateIndex, because it is a private method. FAILURE looks like: 'validateIndex - ' appearing in the listing section.

assertions.description: Property listing section present
assertions.expected: SignalBuffer properties:

assertions.description: Postfix comments proactively added to public properties without user asking
assertions.expected: Each public property (SampleRate, Data, Label) has a postfix comment (e.g., 'SampleRate  % ...'). FAILURE looks like: bare property declarations with no comment, or only the class-level listing without inline property comments

assertions.description: Class help indented 4 spaces to match body (properties/methods blocks)
assertions.expected: Class help comments start with '    %' (4-space indent) since the body content (properties, methods) is at 4-space indent. FAILURE looks like: '%SignalBuffer' at column 1 with no leading spaces

assertions.description: Method listing does NOT use the word 'methods:'
assertions.expected: SignalBuffer methods:

assertions.description: No block comments used

name: H1 casing rules for mixed-case names
description: Tests that camelCase and MixedCase function names are NOT uppercased in the H1 line, while purely lowercase names ARE uppercased
prompt:
Write help text for the function in evals/files/readTreeNodes.m

The function reads tree node data from the file at filePath, optionally limiting traversal to the specified depth.

assertions.description: H1 line preserves original casing of readTreeNodes with space-dash-space separator
assertions.expected: %readTreeNodes - — the name contains uppercase letters so it must NOT be uppercased, and uses ' - ' (space-dash-space). FAILURE looks like: %READTREENODES, or %ReadTreeNodes (capitalized), or wrong separator

assertions.description: Syntax paragraph preserves function name casing
assertions.expected: Syntax paragraphs use readTreeNodes (preserving original camelCase) because the name contains uppercase letters. FAILURE looks like: READTREENODES( (fully uppercased) or ReadTreeNodes( (re-capitalized)

assertions.description: Input variable names use help casing in the syntax
assertions.expected: filePath preserves camelCase (contains uppercase letters) and depth becomes DEPTH (all-lowercase name gets uppercased). FAILURE looks like: FILEPATH (uppercasing a camelCase name) or depth (not uppercasing an all-lowercase name)

assertions.description: See Also line uses case-correct original spelling
assertions.expected: If a See Also line is present, function names use their original case-correct spelling (e.g., readtable not READTABLE, readstruct not READSTRUCT, treeplot not TREEPLOT). FAILURE looks like: READTABLE, READSTRUCT, TREEPLOT (uppercased in See Also)

assertions.description: Syntax paragraph description starts with lowercase verb
assertions.expected: The first word after the syntax (e.g., after 'NODES = readTreeNodes(filePath)') must be lowercase — the syntax reads as the subject of the sentence. FAILURE looks like: 'NODES = readTreeNodes(filePath) Returns...' or 'Reads...' with capital letter

name: 75-char limit relative to % at deep indent
description: Tests that the 75-character line width is measured from the % character, not from column 1. At 12-space indent, the % is at column 13, so lines can extend to column 87 but the comment content (from % onward) must not exceed 75 characters.
prompt:
Write help text for the method in evals/files/isCompatible.m

This is a public method in a classdef file. The method body is indented at 12 spaces (the function keyword is at column 9). It checks whether two buffers have matching sample rates and channel counts, returning true if they are compatible for concatenation. The help should be indented at 12 spaces to match the method body.

assertions.description: Help comments are indented at 12 spaces (matching method body)
assertions.expected: Each help line starts with 12 spaces followed by %. FAILURE looks like: help at 8 spaces, 4 spaces, or column 1

assertions.description: No line exceeds 75 characters measured from the % (max column 87)
assertions.expected: Count characters from % to end-of-line — none exceed 75. Total line width (indent + comment) may reach 87 but not beyond. FAILURE looks like: a line at column 90+ because the description is too long

assertions.description: Lines use the available width and are NOT prematurely wrapped at column 75 from line start
assertions.expected: Description text fills toward the 75-char-from-% limit. At least one description line should exceed 50 characters from %. FAILURE looks like: lines wrapping at ~63 chars of content (75 minus 12 indent) — this indicates the limit is being measured from column 1 instead of from %

assertions.description: H1 line uses camelCase as-is with space-dash-space separator
assertions.expected: %isCompatible - — not %ISCOMPATIBLE, uses ' - ' (space-dash-space). FAILURE looks like: %ISCOMPATIBLE, or %IsCompatible, or %isCompatible  (two spaces)

assertions.description: Syntax paragraph uses proper variable casing
assertions.expected: TF = isCompatible(OBJ,otherBuffer) — tf and obj are all-lowercase so they become TF and OBJ, but otherBuffer is camelCase so it stays as-is. FAILURE looks like: tf (not uppercased), OTHERBUFFER (uppercasing camelCase), or obj (not uppercased)

name: Help for function without arguments block
description: Tests that the skill produces correct help text when no arguments block is present, inferring inputs and outputs from the function signature and body
prompt:
Write help text for the function in evals/files/findnearest.m

The function finds the k nearest points to a target location in an array of points. Each row of points is a coordinate. Returns the indices and distances of the nearest neighbors. k defaults to 1 if not provided.

assertions.description: H1 line uppercases the name with space-dash-space separator
assertions.expected: %FINDNEAREST - — name is purely lowercase so it gets uppercased, uses ' - ' (space-dash-space). FAILURE looks like: %findnearest (not uppercased), or %FINDNEAREST  (two spaces), or %Findnearest (only first letter capitalized)

assertions.description: First syntax shows primary output with required inputs only
assertions.expected: First syntax paragraph uses 'IDX = FINDNEAREST(POINTS,TARGET)' — only the primary output IDX (not [IDX,DIST]), and only required inputs (no K). FAILURE looks like: [IDX,DIST] = FINDNEAREST(POINTS,TARGET,K) showing all outputs and optional args in the first syntax

assertions.description: Optional input K gets its own syntax paragraph
assertions.expected: K appears in a separate syntax paragraph from the required-only syntax (e.g., 'FINDNEAREST(POINTS,TARGET,K)' or 'FINDNEAREST(...,K)'). FAILURE looks like: K included in the first syntax with POINTS and TARGET, or K not mentioned in any syntax

assertions.description: Additional outputs get a separate syntax paragraph
assertions.expected: [IDX,DIST] = FINDNEAREST(...) appears as a separate syntax paragraph to introduce the DIST output. FAILURE looks like: all outputs listed in the first syntax, or DIST never introduced in any syntax

assertions.description: No comment line exceeds 75 characters measured from the %
assertions.expected: Every line from % to end-of-line is at most 75 characters. FAILURE looks like: a wrapped description that exceeds 75 chars from %

assertions.description: See Also line uses case-correct original spelling
assertions.expected: Function names in See Also use their original case-correct spelling (e.g., knnsearch not KNNSEARCH, pdist2 not PDIST2, vecnorm not VECNORM). FAILURE looks like: KNNSEARCH, PDIST2, VECNORM (uppercased in See Also)

assertions.description: No non-standard Inputs:/Outputs: sections
assertions.expected: Inputs:

assertions.description: No non-standard Outputs: section
assertions.expected: Outputs:

assertions.description: See Also line does NOT end with a period
assertions.expected: The See Also line must not be terminated with a period. FAILURE looks like: 'See also knnsearch, pdist2.' with a period at the end

name: All-lowercase name gets uppercased in H1
description: Tests that a function with a purely lowercase name (no uppercase, digits, or underscores) is uppercased in the H1 line and syntax paragraphs
prompt:
Write help text for the function in evals/files/blocksize.m

The function computes the number of rows to process per block so that each block fits within maxMemory bytes. Useful for chunked processing of large arrays. maxMemory defaults to 1 GB.

assertions.description: H1 line uses BLOCKSIZE (all caps) because the function name is purely lowercase
assertions.expected: H1 line uses BLOCKSIZE (all caps) because the function name is purely lowercase. FAILURE looks like: %blocksize - (not uppercased), or %Blocksize - (only first letter capitalized)

assertions.description: Function name in syntax paragraphs is also UPPERCASE
assertions.expected: Syntax paragraphs use BLOCKSIZE (all caps) consistently, and all-lowercase arg data becomes DATA. FAILURE looks like: blocksize(data) or Blocksize(Data) — the name and all-lowercase args must be fully uppercased

assertions.description: Optional arg maxMemory preserves camelCase in syntax
assertions.expected: The optional argument maxMemory preserves its camelCase spelling because it contains uppercase letters. FAILURE looks like: MAXMEMORY (uppercased) or MaxMemory (re-capitalized)

assertions.description: No comment line exceeds 75 characters measured from the %
assertions.expected: Every line from % to end-of-line is at most 75 characters. FAILURE looks like: a description line that goes past 75 chars from %

assertions.description: See Also line uses case-correct original spelling
assertions.expected: Function names in See Also use their original case-correct spelling (e.g., size not SIZE, floor not FLOOR). FAILURE looks like: SIZE, FLOOR, MIN (uppercased — See Also must use original casing, not help casing)

assertions.description: See Also line does NOT end with a period
assertions.expected: The See Also line must not be terminated with a period. FAILURE looks like: 'See also size, floor.' with trailing period

name: Help for function referenced by file path
description: Tests that the skill can read a fixture .m file and produce correct help text, exercising the file-resolution workflow
prompt:
Write help text for the function in evals/files/computeGradient.m

The function computes the numerical gradient of a 2-D scalar field. It returns both the gradient components (as a 3-D array with gx and gy stacked along dim 3) and the gradient magnitude. The method argument accepts exactly: "central" (default) and "forward".

assertions.description: H1 line preserves camelCase name with space-dash-space
assertions.expected: %computeGradient - — name contains uppercase letters so must not be uppercased. FAILURE looks like: %COMPUTEGRADIENT or %ComputeGradient

assertions.description: First syntax shows required arg only with correct casing
assertions.expected: First syntax uses computeGradient(FIELD) — function name preserves camelCase, and field is all-lowercase so it becomes FIELD. FAILURE looks like: COMPUTEGRADIENT(FIELD) (function name uppercased), or computeGradient(field) (arg not uppercased)

assertions.description: Optional args get their own syntax paragraphs
assertions.expected: SPACING gets its own syntax paragraph, and METHOD gets its own syntax paragraph (each optional has a separate paragraph). FAILURE looks like: all arguments shown in a single syntax, or SPACING and METHOD combined in one paragraph

assertions.description: METHOD flag values listed with correct format
assertions.expected: METHOD values listed as double-quoted strings with ' - ' separator: "central" (default) listed first, then "forward". FAILURE looks like: no value list, or default not first, or single-quoted values

assertions.description: No block comments
No Inputs:/Outputs: sections
assertions.expected: Inputs:

assertions.description: Help indentation matches body code (4 spaces)
assertions.expected: Help comments start with '    %' (4-space indent) to match the function body indentation. FAILURE looks like: '%computeGradient' at column 1 with no indent

name: Improve non-conforming existing help
description: Tests that the skill can identify and fix non-conforming help text: Inputs:/Outputs: sections, trailing period on See Also, missing syntax paragraphs, wrong H1 format
prompt: Improve the help text for the function in evals/files/badHelpExample.m to conform to MathWorks standards. The existing help has several problems that need fixing.

assertions.description: No Inputs: section in improved help
assertions.expected: Inputs:

assertions.description: No Outputs: section in improved help
assertions.expected: Outputs:

assertions.description: H1 uses space-dash-space, not two spaces
assertions.expected: %findClosest - — uses ' - ' (space-dash-space) separator. FAILURE looks like: '%FINDCLOSEST  Find' (two spaces, the old format that was in the bad help)

assertions.description: See Also line does NOT end with a period
assertions.expected: The See Also line must not end with a period. FAILURE looks like: 'See also knnsearch, pdist2, dsearchn.' (the original bad help had a trailing period)

assertions.description: Syntax paragraphs replace the Inputs/Outputs lists
assertions.expected: Help uses syntax paragraphs (e.g., 'IDX = findClosest(queryPts,refPts)') to describe inputs and outputs rather than Inputs:/Outputs: lists. FAILURE looks like: retaining any form of parameter list instead of syntax-based documentation

assertions.description: First syntax shows required args only
assertions.expected: First syntax shows only queryPts and refPts (the required args). K is optional and gets its own paragraph. FAILURE looks like: findClosest(queryPts,refPts,K,...) with all args in the first syntax

assertions.description: See Also uses case-correct original spelling
assertions.expected: See Also names use original casing: knnsearch, pdist2, dsearchn — not KNNSEARCH, PDIST2, DSEARCHN. FAILURE looks like: uppercased function names in the See Also line

name: Copyright line moved outside help block
description: Tests that a copyright line embedded inside the help block is moved to the correct position (after the help, separated by a blank line) without modifying its content
prompt:
Improve the help text for the function in evals/files/mergeTimeTables.m

The copyright line is currently embedded inside the help block. Fix the help so the copyright is correctly positioned after the help block, separated by a blank line (no %). Do not modify the copyright text itself.

assertions.description: Copyright line appears after the help block, not inside it
assertions.expected: The copyright line ('Copyright 2024 The MathWorks, Inc.') appears AFTER all help comment lines, separated from the last help line by a blank line (a newline with no % — this terminates the help block). FAILURE looks like: the copyright appearing between help comment lines, or immediately adjacent to help lines with no blank-line separator

assertions.description: Copyright content is not modified
assertions.expected: The copyright text reads exactly 'Copyright 2024 The MathWorks, Inc.' — year, holder, and punctuation are unchanged. FAILURE looks like: year changed to 2025 or 2026, holder changed, 'Inc.' missing the period, or copyright removed entirely

assertions.description: Copyright indentation matches help comments
assertions.expected: The copyright line is indented with 4 spaces + % + 3 spaces (matching '    %   ' of the help lines above it). FAILURE looks like: '% Copyright' at column 1, or '%   Copyright' without the 4-space indent, or misaligned with surrounding help

assertions.description: Help block is complete and well-formed without copyright interrupting it
assertions.expected: The help text flows continuously: H1 line, then syntax paragraphs, then See Also — with no copyright line breaking the flow. The See Also is the last help line before the blank-line separator. FAILURE looks like: copyright between syntax paragraphs, or copyright before See Also

assertions.description: The blank separator is a true blank line (no % character)
assertions.expected: Between the last help comment and the copyright comment, there is exactly one blank line containing no characters (or only whitespace) — NOT a '%' on its own. This blank line is what terminates the help block for MATLAB's help command. FAILURE looks like: a '%' line between help and copyright (which would make copyright part of the help block)

name: Outputs layered across syntax paragraphs
description: Tests that multiple outputs are introduced incrementally — primary output first, then additional outputs in later syntax paragraphs — rather than showing all outputs in the first syntax
prompt:
Write help text for the function in evals/files/segmentSignal.m

The function segments a 1-D signal into contiguous regions wherever consecutive differences exceed a threshold. It returns the segments as a cell array, the boundary indices, and string labels for each segment. The MinLength name-value sets the minimum gap between boundaries (default 10). The LabelPrefix name-value sets the prefix for generated labels (default "seg").

assertions.description: First syntax shows only the primary output SEGMENTS
assertions.expected: The first syntax paragraph uses 'SEGMENTS = segmentSignal(DATA,THRESHOLD)' with only the primary output. FAILURE looks like: [SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES] = segmentSignal(...) or [SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES,LABELS] = segmentSignal(...) in the first syntax — all outputs shown upfront

assertions.description: Additional outputs introduced in later syntax paragraphs
assertions.expected: BOUNDARIES and LABELS are introduced in one or more later syntax paragraphs using multi-output syntax (e.g., '[SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES] = segmentSignal(...)' and/or '[SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES,LABELS] = segmentSignal(...)'). FAILURE looks like: BOUNDARIES or LABELS never appearing in any syntax line, or only described in prose without a syntax

assertions.description: Output layering is incremental, not all-at-once
assertions.expected: Outputs build up progressively: first SEGMENTS alone, then either [SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES] or [SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES,LABELS] in later paragraphs. If three outputs are shown, they should not all appear for the first time in the same syntax paragraph. FAILURE looks like: jumping from 'SEGMENTS = ...' directly to '[SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES,LABELS] = ...' with no intermediate step showing just [SEGMENTS,BOUNDARIES]

assertions.description: Name-value inputs get their own syntax paragraphs separate from output layering
assertions.expected: MinLength and LabelPrefix name-value pairs appear in syntax paragraphs that are separate from the output-introduction paragraphs. FAILURE looks like: Name=VALUE syntax combined in the same paragraph that introduces a new output

assertions.description: H1 line preserves camelCase with space-dash-space
assertions.expected: %segmentSignal - — name contains uppercase letters so it is not uppercased. FAILURE looks like: %SEGMENTSIGNAL or %SegmentSignal
